neighborhoods.name: Stari Grad (Old Town)
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO-listed walled city with marble streets, baroque churches, and dramatic Adriatic views
neighborhoods.character: Historic walled, car-free, tourist-heavy in summer

housing.predominantType: Restored stone apartments within medieval walls

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Dubrovnik. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Lapad
neighborhoods.description: Peninsula neighborhood west of old town with beaches, promenades, and a more residential feel

housing.predominantType: Apartments, small houses, and hotel conversions

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Dubrovnik's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Gruz
neighborhoods.description: Port area with the main harbor, market, and more affordable housing options
neighborhoods.character: Working port area, local market hub, practical

housing.predominantType: Apartments in socialist-era and newer buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Ploce
neighborhoods.description: Eastern area near the old town with luxury hotels, Banje Beach, and stunning fortification views
neighborhoods.character: Upscale, scenic, close to old town attractions

housing.predominantType: Luxury apartments and renovated stone houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
